system.description: Make an opposed Charm or Deception versus Discipline check targeting a single non-nemesis adversary within medium range. If successful, the target cannot attack you, or perform hostile actions against you, until the end of their next turn. You may spend two Advantage to increase the length of the effect by one additional turn, and spend one Triumph to extend the benefit to all of their identified allies within short range. The effect ends immediately if you or a known ally attacks the target. Your GM may rule that some targets are immune to this ability.

After you inflict a Critical Injury with a ranged weapon and roll the result, you may suffer 2 strain to use this talent and select any Critical Injury of the same severity to apply to the target instead.
Each rank of Defensive increases your melee defense and ranged defense by one.

Increases the defense of any vehicle you pilot by one per rank of Defensive Driving. Requires the optional vehicle rules; without them, this talent instead adds one Setback per rank to combat checks targeting your vehicle, or you while piloting it.
Each rank of Enduring increases your soak value by one.

Requires the Field Commander talent to benefit from this talent. When you use the Field Commander talent, you affect a number of allies equal to twice your Presence. You may also spend one Triumph to allow one ally to suffer 1 strain to perform an action instead of a maneuver.

Once per session, make a Hard (3 Difficulty) Mechanics check. If successful, one device involved in the current encounter (subject to your GM's approval) spontaneously fails, whether through your actions or simply convenient timing.
Requires the Inspiring Rhetoric talent to benefit from this talent. You may suffer 1 strain to use the Inspiring Rhetoric talent as a maneuver instead of an action.

Once per encounter, make a Hard (3 Difficulty) Mechanics check and choose one of your cybernetic implants that grants +1 to a characteristic rating, +1 rank to a skill, or +1 rank of a ranked talent. If successful, until the end of the encounter the chosen cybernetic instead provides +2 to the affected characteristic rating (to a maximum of 7), skill (to a maximum of 5), or ranked talent. Your GM may spend a Despair, or three Threat, from the check to have the overcharged cybernetic short out at the end of the encounter; it provides no benefit until you spend several hours making an Average (2 Difficulty) Mechanics check to repair it.
Requires the Scathing Tirade talent to benefit from this talent. You may suffer 1 strain to use the Scathing Tirade talent as a maneuver instead of an action.

An enhanced Flurry. Before making a ranged combat check with a pistol, suffer 1 strain to use this talent. Make one additional attack against the same target using the same weapon. The Setback dice from Flurry are removed.
system.source: Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic
